original_abstract: In this paper we introduce a new game to crowd-source natural language referring expressions.By designing a two player game, we can both collect and verify referring expressions directly within the game.To date, the game has produced a dataset containing 130,525 expressions, referring to 96,654 distinct objects, in 19,894 photographs of natural scenes.This dataset is larger and more varied than previous REG datasets and allows us to study referring expressions in real-world scenes.We provide an in depth analysis of the resulting dataset.Based on our findings, we design a new optimization based model for generating referring expressions and perform experimental evaluations on 3 test sets.
humanized_abstract: We present a game to collect natural language referring expressions using a two-player game. Our game design enables us to collect and verify referring expressions that a user provides, while remaining an entertaining activity. Using the game, we collected a new large scale Referring Expression GAmes (REG) dataset with 130,525 referring expressions for 96,654 objects and on 19,894 images collected from diverse natural scenes. Our dataset is by a large margin bigger and diverse than any previous REG benchmark and allows us to perform the first analysis on expressions collected from images of everyday life. Our analysis allows us to design an optimization based model that simulates the process by which players produce referring expressions. The method is evaluated on 3 different benchmark REG datasets.

Our analysis allows us to design an optimization based model that simulates the process by which players produce referring expressions. The method is evaluated on 3 different benchmark REG datasets.
